name: Schofield
description_short: Schofield Federal Credit Union is a military-focused credit union established in 1951, serving over 2,100 members at Schofield Barracks, Hawaii with $32.6M in assets.
description_long:
Schofield Federal Credit Union was established in 1951 and is headquartered at Schofield Barracks in Hawaii. The credit union primarily serves military personnel and organizations, with a specific mission to address the financial needs of military communities. Over seven decades, it has built a member base of more than 2,100 loyal customers and manages approximately $32.6 million in total assets.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of banking and lending products tailored to its membership. Members can access checking accounts (Share Draft accounts with 800+ members), savings accounts (Regular Savings with 1,700+ members), certificates of deposit (200+ members), and money market accounts. Loan products include auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, and mortgage options with both fixed-rate and adjustable-rate structures. Additional services include IRAs/KEOGH accounts, wire transfers, online bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and financial counseling.

Schofield FCU distinguishes itself through its exclusive focus on military membership and tailored financial products for military personnel. The credit union operates a single branch location at 331 Brannon Road in Schofield Barracks and maintains one ATM for member access. The institution emphasizes financial education and member support, offering financial calculators, a financial tip dashboard, and student scholarships. Members can access services through online banking and dedicated mobile apps for both iOS and Android platforms.

A significant caveat is that the institution currently reports service disruptions—the website notes the lobby is "currently closed" and the organization "may be experiencing service issues." Additionally, there are no customer reviews available on the platform, making it difficult to assess member satisfaction independently. The single-branch model and limited service availability create operational constraints for members needing in-person assistance. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established 1951 with 70+ years of credit union history and stability
Serves exclusively military personnel with mission-aligned financial products
Offers both fixed-rate and adjustable-rate mortgages in addition to auto and personal loans
Provides mobile banking apps for iOS and Android with online bill pay and remote deposit capture
Includes financial counseling and educational resources plus student scholarship programs
Maintains competitive rates typical of credit unions versus traditional banks
Comprehensive account types including IRAs, certificates of deposit, and money market accounts
cons: Website indicates lobby is currently closed and service issues may be ongoing, creating access and reliability concerns
Single branch location limits convenience for members outside Schofield Barracks area
No customer reviews available to verify actual member experience or satisfaction
Membership restricted to military-affiliated individuals, excluding general public
Limited asset base ($32.6M) compared to larger regional credit unions may constrain loan products and rates
best_for: Active duty military personnel stationed at Schofield Barracks or with military affiliation
Military families seeking accounts and loans with military-focused products and financial counseling
Service members interested in auto loans, mortgages, and personal loans with credit union rates
diagnosis: Schofield Federal Credit Union is best suited for military-affiliated individuals stationed at or near Schofield Barracks, Hawaii who need comprehensive banking and lending services within a credit union framework. The primary caveat is that current service disruptions (closed lobby, reported service issues) and limited geographic accessibility (single branch) may create operational challenges for members, and the lack of public reviews makes independent verification of service quality impossible.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
